title: Corylus sutchuenensis
intro: Corylus sutchuenensis is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the family Betulaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Corylus sutchuenensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Corylus sutchuenensis include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support digestive health.
taxonomy: Corylus sutchuenensis is a species of hazelnut tree, classified within the family Betulaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Corylus, which comprises various species of hazelnut trees. Corylus sutchuenensis is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions. It is a deciduous tree, characterized by its small, edible nuts and compound leaves. The plant's classification is further supported by its similarity in characteristics to other Corylus species.
